title: Binary expressions should not always return the same value
description: Binary expressions should not always return the same valueAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugComparisons that always evaluate to true or to false, logical expressions that either always or never short-circuit and comparisons to a newly constructed object should not be used.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?An expression that always produces the same result, regardless of the inputs, is unnecessary and likely indicates a programmerâ€™s error. This can come fromconfusing operator precedenceexpecting strict equality between different typesexpecting objects to be compared by valueexpecting empty objects to befalseornullmistyping>=forâ‡’This can also happen when you put an assignment in a logical sub-expression. While not strictly a bug, this practice is confusing and should be
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Avoid redundant binary expressions - variable compared with itself always returns the same result.
